title: How Modern Lighting Is Damaging Your Health (ft. Tristan Scott)

chunks.title: Introduction to Light and Health
chunks.summary: The podcast opens with a discussion about the importance of light, particularly blue light, and its effects on health. The host expresses concerns about modern lighting environments, particularly the prevalence of 'junk light' and electromagnetic fields that negatively impact human health.

chunks.title: Nutrient Deficiencies from Light Exposure
chunks.summary: Tristan discusses how light, specifically infrared light, is one of the most overlooked nutrient deficiencies in modern society. They elaborate on the importance of morning sunlight and how it impacts circadian rhythms and overall health.
chunks.transcript: perhaps one of the most under thought about underrecognized nutrient deficiencies in our population today yeah I think at a high level it's it's light and you can break that down into what spectrum of light it is and for me that's it's probably infrared Nu UV but at a high level I would say maybe even more important that that infrared light is one of the biggest nutrient deficiencies because of our modern indoor environment and and what we did in the morning today is vitally important for many different reasons and we got out there right after we woke up the sun you know hadn't even crested The Horizon so we're giving our body all the right input signals we're getting that you know high-intensity brightness compared to an indoor environment the blue light in fact is is waking our body up it's giving you know the Circadian mechanism of it's morning time let's release cortisol and then we're also getting a swath of the rest of the spectrum the infrared like component which is really removed from our modern indoor environment so it's multifaceted I would say but for me that's one of the easiest most important things you can do

chunks.title: The Role of Melatonin
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to melatonin production, explaining that while melatonin is often viewed as a hormone produced by the pineal gland, most of it is created at the cellular level throughout the body. This understanding highlights the significance of light exposure in regulating melatonin and its antioxidant properties.

chunks.title: Nutrition and Light Connection
chunks.summary: The speakers explore the connection between diet and light exposure, noting that poor dietary choices, especially related to processed foods and seed oils, can lead to adverse health effects. They advocate for a return to whole, nutrient-dense foods to break the cycle of chronic illness.

chunks.title: Electromagnetic Fields (EMF)
chunks.summary: The discussion delves into the topic of electromagnetic fields (EMFs), highlighting how they are an unseen threat to health. Tristan explains the differences between electric fields and radio frequency fields, detailing the biological implications of long-term exposure to these energies.

chunks.title: Conclusion and Call to Action
chunks.summary: The podcast concludes with a call to prioritize natural light exposure, minimize artificial light and EMF exposure, and make informed choices about nutrition. They encourage listeners to reclaim their health by connecting more with the outdoors and making healthier lifestyle choices.
